qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated local HTTP API permits PTY command execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets missing authentication on the loopback `/pty` control-plane endpoint, while the advisory targets the web UI `?url=` override plus unsanitized markdown-to-HTML rendering that yields same-origin XSS on `localhost:4096` before `/pty` is reached.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable web UI code path. The advisory requires attacker-controlled remote session content to be rendered into the localhost origin through `packages/app/src/app.tsx` server URL override behavior and unsanitized HTML insertion in `packages/ui/src/components/markdown.tsx`, whereas the qualifying finding is a separate localhost API authentication issue.
evidence: The advisory describes a malicious website using `?url=` to make the localhost UI load attacker-controlled session data, then gaining same-origin JavaScript execution because markdown-rendered HTML is inserted into the DOM without sanitization.
`packages/app/src/app.tsx` reads `new URLSearchParams(document.location.search).get("url")` and uses it as the default server URL for the web UI.
`packages/ui/src/components/markdown.tsx` renders parsed markdown with `innerHTML={html.latest}`, which is the advisory's XSS-enabling sink.

The qualifying finding instead cites `packages/opencode/src/server/server.ts` and `packages/opencode/src/pty/index.ts` for unauthenticated loopback access to `/pty`, without identifying the advisory's remote-session loading path or the markdown/XSS root cause.
